questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics. Reading $\mu_{max}$ (plateau) and the half-saturation constant $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate at substrate $S=100\,\text{mg/L}$ is _____ h⁻¹.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Monod: $\mu=\dfrac{\mu_{max}S}{K_s+S}$, with $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.5\times100}{50+100}=0.333.$$

**Answer.** $0.333\,\text{h}^{-1}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: An enzyme follows Michaelis–Menten kinetics. Reading $V_{max}$ (plateau) and $K_m$ (half-saturation) from the curve shown, the reaction velocity at substrate concentration $[S]=15\,\text{mmol/L}$ is _____ µmol·L⁻¹·min⁻¹.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Michaelis–Menten: $v=\dfrac{V_{max}[S]}{K_m+[S]}$, with $V_{max}$ and $K_m$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{10\times15}{5+15}=7.5.$$

**Answer.** $7.5\,\mu\text{mol L}^{-1}\text{min}^{-1}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: In a reactor, $40\,\text{mg/L}$ of biomass is produced while $100\,\text{mg/L}$ of substrate is consumed. The observed yield $Y_{X/S}$ is _____ mg/mg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Y_{X/S}=\dfrac{\Delta X}{\Delta S}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{40}{100}=0.4.$$

**Answer.** $0.4$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Bacteria grow exponentially with specific growth rate $\mu=0.231\,\text{h}^{-1}$. The doubling (generation) time is _____ hours.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $t_d=\dfrac{\ln 2}{\mu}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.231}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{h}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Drinking-water standards require E. coli to be ABSENT in any 100 mL sample. A membrane-filtration test of a 100 mL sample of treated water yields 8 E. coli colonies. The correct interpretation is that the water
options: fails the potability standard and is unsafe to drink
is potable, since 8 is a low count
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The standard is zero E. coli per 100 mL; 8 colonies/100 mL exceeds it, signalling recent faecal contamination — the water is unsafe.

**Working.**
Compare the measured count to the zero-tolerance standard.

**Answer.** Fails / unsafe

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: In a nitrifying reactor the ammonia-nitrogen falls from $40\,\text{mg/L}$ to $6\,\text{mg/L}$ as it is oxidised to nitrate. Taking the stoichiometric requirement as $4.57\,\text{g O}_2$ per g of N nitrified, the oxygen demand exerted by nitrification is _____ mg/L.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Nitrification (NH₄⁺ → NO₃⁻) consumes 4.57 g O₂ per g N. Demand $=\Delta N\times4.57$.

**Working.**
$$(40-6)\times4.57=155.38\,\text{mg/L}.$$

**Answer.** $155.38\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Which processes are strictly anaerobic in wastewater treatment?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Methanogenesis requires strict anaerobiosis. Denitrification is anoxic (uses NO₃⁻), nitrification and carbon oxidation are aerobic.

**Working.**
Only methanogenesis is strictly anaerobic.

**Answer.** Methanogenesis
